narrative.headline: What 2014 Hyundai Sonata owners report most
narrative.summary: Engine complaints dominate owner reports, with some incidents involving fires or crashes. Electrical system faults and exterior lighting malfunctions each account for a substantial share of complaints, and steering behavior also draws significant concern. Across all areas, owners describe symptoms that recur and that dealers sometimes struggle to diagnose or resolve.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Owners report the engine or transmission failing to shift properly at low speeds, with some incidents escalating to fires or crashes. · 25 fire · 6 crash · 7 injured

issues.sample: CAR BATTERY GOES DEAD,UNABLE TO START CAR, COULD HAPPEN AT ANY TIME.  THIS HAS HAPPENED 3 TIMES.  THE DEALER HAS NO IDEA WHY.  EVEN HYUNDAI CORP DOES NOT HAVE ANY ANSWERS.  THE CAR HAS BEEN BACK TO THE DEALER FOR DAYS AT A TIME WITH NO RESOLUTION.  I FEEL THIS IS A SAFETY CONCERN
issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Owners report the battery dying unexpectedly and repeatedly, leaving the car unable to start, with neither dealers nor the manufacturer able to identify a cause. · 9 fire · 1 crash

issues.title: Exterior Lighting
issues.blurb: Owners report turn signals operating visually but producing no audible chime, along with other chime-based alerts going silent. · 5 fire · 1 crash

issues.title: Steering
issues.blurb: Owners report the vehicle drifting or wandering side to side at freeway speeds rather than tracking straight, with some associated crashes. · 2 crash · 1 injured
